data.description:

Entravision ( (EVC) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 16, 2026, Entravision Communications Corporation disclosed that director Lara Sweet informed the Board she will not stand for reelection at the company’s 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. She will continue to serve on the Board, as well as on the Audit and Compensation Committees, until the meeting date.

The company stated that Sweet’s decision was solely for personal reasons and did not result from any disagreement over Entravision’s operations, policies, or practices. The announcement signals a forthcoming change in Board composition while indicating no underlying governance or strategic dispute behind her planned departure.

The most recent analyst rating on (EVC) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Entravision stock, see the EVC Stock Forecast page.

Technicals are moderately supportive with price above major moving averages, while valuation is mixed—an attractive dividend yield but negative earnings.

data.title: Ashford Hospitality Sells La Posada Hotel, Reduces Debt
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ashford Hospitality ( (AHT) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 17, 2026, Ashford Hospitality Trust completed the sale of the 157-room La Posada de Santa Fe hotel in Santa Fe, New Mexico, for approximately $56.8 million in cash, net of selling expenses, through its indirect subsidiary Ashford Posada LP. The company used roughly $56.0 million of the proceeds to pay down a mortgage secured by La Posada and another hotel, and issued unaudited pro forma financials showing the removal of La Posada’s assets, liabilities, and operating results, including a preliminary, non-recurring gain on the disposition.

The transaction reflects Ashford Hospitality Trust’s continued portfolio reshaping and balance sheet management, as paying down the mortgage debt tied to the asset reduces leverage while crystallizing a one-time gain from the sale. Stakeholders are provided with updated pro forma financial information for 2024 and the nine months ended September 30, 2025, illustrating how the company’s financial position and performance would appear without La Posada, though the company noted the gain and related tax effects remain subject to final adjustment.

The most recent analyst rating on (AHT) stock is a Hold with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ashford Hospitality stock, see the AHT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AHT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AHT is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance, especially negative equity/high leverage and sustained losses, which outweigh improving free cash flow. Technicals are modestly positive in the short term (price above 20/50-day averages and positive MACD), but valuation remains challenged due to negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on AHT stock, click here.

More about Ashford Hospitality

Ashford Hospitality Trust, Inc. is a real estate investment trust focused on owning and investing in hotel properties. Through its subsidiaries, the company holds interests in upscale and upper-upscale hotels across key U.S. markets, generating revenue primarily from lodging operations and related hospitality assets.

data.title: Banco Bradesco Shareholders Approve BRL 6.67 Billion Capital Increase and New Management Profit-Sharing Rules
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Banco Bradesco SA ( (BBD) ) has issued an update.

On March 10, 2026, Banco Bradesco held a special shareholders’ meeting that approved a BRL 6.67 billion increase in its stock capital, raising it from BRL 87.1 billion to BRL 93.77 billion through the capitalization of legal profit reserves without issuing new shares. The meeting also endorsed corresponding amendments to the bylaws to reflect the new capital amount and introduced provisions authorizing profit-sharing payments to management at the discretion of the board of directors, signaling a shift in Bradesco’s governance and compensation framework that may affect how profits are allocated between executives and shareholders.

The detailed distance voting results showed broad shareholder support for the capital increase and related bylaw adjustment, while opinions were more divided on allowing profit-sharing for management. These changes strengthen Bradesco’s capital structure via internal resources and formalize a mechanism for variable executive remuneration, which could influence future earnings distribution policy and align management incentives with performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (BBD) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Banco Bradesco SA stock, see the BBD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BBD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BBD is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weaker financial performance quality—especially highly volatile and negative recent operating/free cash flow and the sharp revenue decline—despite ongoing profitability. Offsetting factors include constructive technical momentum, a reasonable value/dividend setup, and an earnings call narrative supporting continued growth and improving efficiency, albeit with cost and capital/regulatory risks.

To see Spark’s full report on BBD stock, click here.

More about Banco Bradesco SA

Banco Bradesco S.A. is one of Brazil’s largest publicly held banking institutions, offering a broad range of financial services including retail and corporate banking, credit, insurance and asset management. The bank serves individuals and companies across Brazil and abroad, playing a key role in the country’s financial system and capital markets.

American Shared Hospital Services ( (AMS) ) has provided an update.

American Shared Hospital Services has amended its long-standing Proton Beam Radiation Therapy Lease Agreement with Orlando Health, originally executed in 2006 and previously modified in 2012. On March 13, 2026, the parties agreed to extend the lease for an additional seven years, from April 6, 2026 through April 5, 2033, with lease payments during the extended term tied to a technical component collection percentage that will decrease in certain twelve-month periods.

The amendment also clarifies key operational and financial arrangements related to the leased proton therapy system, including granting Orlando Health an option to purchase the equipment at the end of the lease, with a defined price and exercise window. It further outlines the company’s responsibility and related financial terms for removing the equipment if the purchase option is not exercised, as well as updated maintenance and insurance obligations, providing more certainty for both parties over the long-term use and disposition of the asset.

An update from Tri-Continental Corp ( (TY) ) is now available.

On March 19, 2026, Tri-Continental Corporation’s board announced that the company’s 96th Annual Meeting of Stockholders is scheduled for June 16, 2026, in Minneapolis, Minnesota. The board set April 21, 2026, as the record date for determining stockholders entitled to receive notice of, and vote at, the meeting or any postponement or adjournment.

At the meeting, shareholders will vote on electing four directors—Brian J. Gallagher, Ryan C. Larrenaga, Nancy T. Lukitsh, and Catherine James Paglia—to terms extending to the 2029 annual meeting, and on ratifying PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as independent auditor for the 2026 fiscal year. These governance decisions will shape the company’s board composition and audit oversight for the coming years, with full details to appear in the forthcoming proxy materials to be filed with regulators.

data.title: Icon Energy Lifts Q1 2026 Revenue Outlook and Bolsters Liquidity Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Icon Energy Corp. ( (ICON) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 19, 2026, Icon Energy Corp. reported that its three-vessel dry bulk fleet is fully employed on index-linked time charters tied to the Baltic Panamax and Supramax indices, aiming to balance high utilization with exposure to freight rate upside. The company highlighted that none of its ships are currently operating in or heading toward the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened U.S.-Iran tensions, while it continues to monitor geopolitical risks and prioritize crew and vessel safety.

For the first quarter of 2026, Icon expects average gross hire of about $14,000 per day, up 63% from $8,600 in the prior-year quarter, supported by the addition of the Ultramax vessel M/V Charlie in June 2025, which is set to lift Operating Days by 50% year on year. As a result, revenue, net for the quarter is projected between $3.5 million and $3.7 million, more than double the $1.5 million recorded in the first quarter of 2025.

Management noted that recent disruptions and uncertainty around commodity flows from the Middle East could tighten vessel availability and support ton-mile demand for dry bulk shipping, potentially underpinning stronger freight markets. While the broader impact of the conflict remains unclear, the company sees the current environment as conducive to improved earnings potential for well-positioned dry bulk owners.

To support growth and strengthen liquidity, Icon disclosed that it has raised $6.9 million in net equity proceeds so far in 2026 through its at-the-market program and a standby equity purchase agreement. The new shares were issued at an average price of $2.82, representing a 188% premium to the last reported Nasdaq Capital Market closing price of its common stock on March 18, 2026, signaling robust pricing despite ongoing market volatility.

The most recent analyst rating on (ICON) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Icon Energy Corp. stock, see the ICON Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ICON Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ICON is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance: recent losses, sharply higher leverage, and deeply negative free cash flow outweigh improving revenue. Technicals add downside risk as the stock remains in a clear downtrend with negative momentum. Valuation provides little support given the missing P/E and an unusually high reported dividend yield that appears unreliable based on the provided data.

To see Spark’s full report on ICON stock, click here.

More about Icon Energy Corp.

Icon Energy Corp. is an Athens-based international shipping company that provides worldwide seaborne transportation services for dry bulk cargoes via a fleet of oceangoing vessels. The company charters its Panamax, Kamsarmax and Ultramax ships mainly on index-linked or fixed-rate time charters and voyage charters, and its shares trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol ICON.

Average Trading Volume: 1,031,204

data.title: Scinai Immunotherapeutics Faces Nasdaq Minimum Bid Price Deficiency, Given Until September to Regain Compliance
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Scinai Immunotherapeutics ( (SCNI) ) is now available.

On March 16, 2026, Scinai Immunotherapeutics disclosed that it had received a Nasdaq notice on March 12, 2026 stating its American Depositary Shares had traded below the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days, putting it out of compliance with Nasdaq Capital Market rules. The notification does not immediately affect the listing or trading of the shares, and Scinai has until September 8, 2026 to regain compliance by lifting its ADS closing price to at least $1.00 for ten straight business days, during which it will monitor its stock and consider options to maintain its Nasdaq listing.

The company emphasized that it intends to use the 180‑day grace period provided under Nasdaq’s rules to address the deficiency and avoid potential delisting, a key concern for investors and partners relying on its continued access to U.S. capital markets. Maintaining compliance is important for Scinai’s visibility and liquidity as it develops immunology therapies and offers contract development and manufacturing services through its CDMO subsidiary.

The most recent analyst rating on (SCNI) stock is a Hold with a $0.98 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Scinai Immunotherapeutics stock, see the SCNI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SCNI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SCNI is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—persistent operating losses and continued cash burn despite recent balance sheet improvement. Technicals are a moderate positive with the stock trading above key short-term averages and mildly positive momentum, while valuation is constrained by a negative P/E and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on SCNI stock, click here.

More about Scinai Immunotherapeutics

The company also operates Scinai Biopharma Services Ltd., a CDMO that provides development and manufacturing services to biotechnology and pharmaceutical firms.

data.title: GSK Wins First U.S. Approval for Lynavoy in PBC-Related Itch, Advancing Hepatology Push
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from GlaxoSmithKline ( (GSK) ).

On 19 March 2026, GSK announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lynavoy (linerixibat), an ileal bile acid transporter inhibitor, as the first medicine in the U.S. specifically indicated for cholestatic pruritus in adults with primary biliary cholangitis, a rare autoimmune liver disease. The decision, underpinned by positive phase III GLISTEN trial data and orphan drug designations, marks GSK’s first liver therapy approval, strengthens its position in hepatology just days after agreeing to license global rights to linerixibat to Alfasigma, and could significantly improve quality of life for PBC patients as regulatory reviews continue in Europe, the U.K., Canada and China.

The most recent analyst rating on (GSK) stock is a Hold with a $58.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock, see the GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is supported primarily by resilient fundamentals (strong profitability and positive cash generation) and a constructive earnings outlook with higher dividend guidance and continued specialty/HIV momentum. These positives are tempered by leverage and recent revenue/FCF volatility, while technically the stock is in a strong uptrend but looks overbought, raising near-term pullback risk.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock, click here.

More about GlaxoSmithKline

GSK plc is a global biopharmaceutical company that develops and commercialises prescription medicines and vaccines, with a growing focus on immune‑mediated and liver diseases. The group is extending its inflammation expertise into hepatology, advancing a pipeline that targets chronic hepatitis B and steatotic liver disease, including metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis and alcohol-associated liver disease.

BiomX ( (PHGE) ) has provided an update.

On March 13, 2026, BiomX amended a warrant originally issued in connection with a December 26, 2025 investment, cutting its term from five years to an expiration date of December 31, 2026 and halving the exercise price from $2.00 to $1.00 per share, while leaving the warrant share count unchanged. The company also updated the formula for cashless exercise to reference the lowest VWAP over the five trading days prior to exercise, a move management says is intended to reduce long-dated warrant overhang, simplify its capital structure, and support a stronger financial profile.

In a March 19, 2026 letter to shareholders, CEO Michael Oster framed the warrant amendment as part of a broader effort to stabilize BiomX’s finances, close high-cost operations such as its Maryland facility, and enforce tighter spending discipline. The company has stepped up engagement with investment banks and institutional investors and is evaluating strategic repositioning opportunities, including potential moves into advanced technology and defense-related markets, in a bid to enhance capital access and long-term value for stakeholders.

data.title: MDJM Faces Nasdaq Suspension After Sub-$0.10 Trading, Moves to OTC as It Seeks Hearing
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from MDJM ( (UOKA) ).

On March 19, 2026, MDJM LTD disclosed that it received a Nasdaq staff determination letter on March 13, 2026, stating its shares would be suspended from the exchange as of the market open on March 20 after the stock traded at or below $0.10 for 10 consecutive business days, triggering Nasdaq’s low-price and prior reverse-split rules. The company, already under a one-year discretionary panel monitor following a November 19, 2025 decision, plans to request a hearing before an independent Nasdaq Hearings Panel while its shares migrate to trading on OTC Markets under ticker UOKA, and it has undertaken a new reverse stock split effective March 16, 2026 as part of efforts to regain listing compliance, leaving investors facing heightened uncertainty about the outcome and future of its Nasdaq listing.

The potential suspension and shift to OTC trading may reduce liquidity and visibility for MDJM’s shareholders even as the company pursues strategic cultural projects and international expansion in the U.K. and Europe. The outcome of the upcoming hearing and the effectiveness of its recent capital measures, including the March 16 reverse split, will be critical in determining whether MDJM can restore full Nasdaq listing status and sustain investor confidence.

More about MDJM

MDJM LTD is a global culture innovation company focused on developing cultural intellectual property, animation production, international licensing, and operating cultural venues. The company is expanding in the U.K. by remodeling properties such as Fernie Castle in Scotland and the Robin Hill Property in England into multi-functional venues that combine fine dining, hospitality, art exhibitions, and cultural exchange events while collaborating with European studios on animated works blending Eastern and Western aesthetics.

Average Trading Volume: 18,066,779

data.title: Acrivon Highlights Positive ACR-368 Data and 2025 Results
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. ( (ACRV) ) is now available.

On March 19, 2026, Acrivon Therapeutics reported fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results, highlighting progress in its registrational-intent Phase 2b trial of ACR-368 in endometrial cancer, including a 52% confirmed overall response rate in serous endometrial cancer versus 22% in non-serous disease. The company has completed exploratory Arm 2 using ACR-368 plus ultra low-dose gemcitabine in biomarker-negative patients, initiated Arm 3 in all-comer serous endometrial cancer, and plans to launch Arm 4 testing ACR-368 monotherapy in biomarker-unselected serous endometrial cancer in 2026, while early Phase 1 data for ACR-2316 show encouraging activity in lung cancer, and cash of $118.6 million as of December 31, 2025 is expected to fund operations into the second quarter of 2027.

Acrivon also advanced its broader precision medicine infrastructure by launching a CLIA-certified laboratory for patient testing and companion diagnostic development, and nominating ACR-6840 as an internally discovered oral CDK11 inhibitor for future clinical development. Financially, the company narrowed its net loss to $19.0 million for the fourth quarter and $77.9 million for 2025, with lower research and development and general and administrative expenses year over year, underscoring disciplined spending as it seeks to strengthen its position in high-unmet-need oncology indications such as serous endometrial cancer and AP3-prioritized solid tumors.

The most recent analyst rating on (ACRV) stock is a Buy with a $8.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. stock, see the ACRV Stock Forecast page.

Lithium Americas reported its audited results for the year ended Dec. 31, 2025, highlighting a strong liquidity position with about $905.6 million in cash and restricted cash, and nearly $1.0 billion in capitalized construction and project costs at its Thacker Pass joint venture. During 2025 and early 2026, the company advanced construction, secured two drawdowns totaling $867 million under its $2.23 billion U.S. Department of Energy loan, raised roughly $590.9 million via at-the-market equity programs, and saw partial conversion of Orion’s convertible notes, collectively de-risking funding for the project.

Thacker Pass Phase 1 progressed rapidly in 2025, with detailed engineering 93% complete, procurement 60% complete, and peak on-site personnel expected to reach about 1,800 workers in late 2026 as the company targets mechanical completion in late 2027. Lithium Americas reaffirmed a 2026 capital expenditure range of $1.3 billion to $1.6 billion, including $1.2 billion to $1.5 billion for Phase 1 construction, while actively managing tariff exposure and supply-chain risks, underscoring the project’s growing importance to U.S. energy security and domestic critical-mineral supply.

data.title: Pelthos Highlights 2025 Results and Portfolio Expansion Progress
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pelthos Therapeutics ( (PTHS) ) has issued an update.

On March 19, 2026, Pelthos Therapeutics reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, highlighting the July 2025 launch of ZELSUVMI, which generated $16.2 million in net sales through year-end and drove a 28% sequential revenue increase to $9.1 million in the fourth quarter. From launch through December 31, 2025, 8,948 units were dispensed from 2,712 unique prescribers, with quarterly volumes up 129% in the fourth quarter versus the third quarter, underscoring strong early uptake despite seasonal softness in molluscum contagiosum treatment.

The company continued to build a pediatric-focused infectious dermatology platform, acquiring XEPI in November 2025 and XEGLYZE in January 2026 to complement ZELSUVMI and broaden its addressable market. Pelthos also bolstered its balance sheet with an $18 million private convertible notes financing in November 2025 and a $50 million senior secured term loan facility in January 2026, drawing $30 million, while reducing quarterly SG&A, narrowing operating losses, and improving adjusted EBITDA as it moves toward positive cash flow and scales its expanded 64-representative sales force.

Pelthos ended 2025 with $18 million in cash, excluding the January 2026 term debt funding, and 8.9 million shares outstanding on an as-converted basis, reflecting a capital strategy geared toward managing dilution as it funds commercial expansion. Management framed the improved operating metrics, portfolio expansion, and added financing capacity as positioning the company to capitalize on large unmet markets in pediatric infectious skin diseases and to leverage existing commercial infrastructure with limited incremental overhead.

The most recent analyst rating on (PTHS) stock is a Buy with a $75.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pelthos Therapeutics stock, see the PTHS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PTHS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PTHS is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (large losses and ongoing cash burn) and a soft technical setup (below key short-to-mid moving averages). Offsetting factors include a constructive earnings update tied to the ZELSUVMI launch and supportive corporate actions that improve funding and expand the commercial portfolio, though valuation support is limited by negative earnings.

To see Spark’s full report on PTHS stock, click here.

More about Pelthos Therapeutics

Pelthos Therapeutics Inc. is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on differentiated cutaneous infectious disease products addressing unmet patient needs. Its lead product, ZELSUVMI, is the first and only prescription therapy approved for at-home treatment of molluscum contagiosum, complemented by XEPI for impetigo and XEGLYZE for head lice, targeting mainly pediatric populations in the U.S.

Based in Durham, N.C., and listed on the NYSE American under the ticker PTHS, Pelthos is building a portfolio of FDA-approved topical treatments for common yet undertreated infectious skin conditions. The company is expanding its U.S. sales force to support commercialization and leverage synergies across its pediatric-focused dermatology franchise.
